old_claim.note: Glassmaking provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because glass apparatus belongs to the upstream distillation_advanced technology rather than to the beverage-spirit application itself.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica defines distilled spirits by distilling fermented agricultural ethanol; apparatus materials are not part of the beverage-category definition.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports distillation of fermented ethanol, not a separate glassmaking dependency for spirits.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Keeping glassmaking direct would duplicate the apparatus-material ancestry already represented through distillation_advanced.

ontology_before: Distillation for Alcoholic Spirits depended directly on both Advanced Distillation and Glassmaking.
ontology_after: Distillation for Alcoholic Spirits depends on Advanced Distillation, which carries apparatus-material ancestry including glassmaking where needed.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: glassmaking is no longer a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to a specific glass-still beverage tradition.
A separate glass_still_spirit_distillation node is added for a narrower production method.
